title: Torque transfer apparatus and methods of use
abstract: A torque transfer apparatus and method of use is described. The apparatus (60) comprises a plurality of elements (62) assembled together to form a ring structure oriented in a plane around a longitudinal axis. The ring structure defines a first engagement surface. A structure (56) defining a second engagement surface is rotatable with respect to the ring structure. The ring structure is operable to be moved between a collapsed condition and an expanded condition by movement of the plurality of elements, and movement between the collapsed condition and the expanded condition engages or disengages the first and second engagement surfaces. The apparatus can be configured to transfer a driving torque (e.g. a clutch) or a braking force (e.g. a torque brake).
claims: Claims Claims: 1. A torque transfer apparatus comprising: a plurality of elements assembled together to form a ring structure oriented in a plane around a longitudinal axis and defining a first engagement surface; a structure defining a second engagement surface, wherein the structure is rotatable with respect to the ring structure; wherein the ring structure is operable to be moved between a collapsed condition and an expanded condition by movement of the plurality of elements on imparting or releasing an axial force, and wherein movement between the collapsed condition and the expanded condition engages or disengages the first and second engagement surfaces. 2. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 1 wherein the first and second engagement surfaces are engaged to transfer a driving torque between the ring structure and the rotatable structure. 3. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the apparatus is a clutch. 4. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the first and second engagement surfaces are configured to be engaged to transfer a braking torque therebetween. 5. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 4, wherein the apparatus is a brake. 6. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the apparatus is normally collapsed, and is be actuated to be expanded. 7. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the apparatus is normally expanded, and is actuated to be collapsed. 8. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the plane of the ring structure is perpendicular to the longitudinal axis. 9. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the first engagement surface is an outer surface of the ring structure. 10. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 9, wherein the first engagement surface is located at or on an expanded outer diameter of the ring structure 1 1. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the first engagement surface is an inner surface of the ring structure. 12. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 1 1 , wherein the first engagement surface is located at or on the collapsed inner diameter of the ring structure. 13. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the first engagement surface is inclined to the longitudinal axis of the apparatus. 14. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 13, wherein the first engagement surface is a conical surface. 15. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the elements are configured to move between their expanded and collapsed conditions in a path which is tangential to a circle described around and concentric with the longitudinal axis. 16. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein each element of the ring structure comprises a first contact surface and second contact surface respectively in abutment with first and second adjacent elements. 17. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 16, wherein the elements are configured to slide relative to one another along their respective contact surfaces. 18. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 16 or claim 17, wherein the first contact surface and/or the second contact surface is oriented tangentially to a circle described around and concentric with the longitudinal axis. 19. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 16 to 18, wherein the first contact surface and the second contact surface are non-parallel. 20. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 16 to 19, wherein the first contact surface and the second contact surface converge towards one another in a direction towards an inner surface of the ring structure 21. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 16 to 20, wherein orientation planes of the first and second contact surfaces of the element are tangential to a circle concentric with the ring structure. 22. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 16 to 21 , wherein the first and second contact surfaces of an element are oriented on first and second planes, which intersect an inner surface of the ring at first and second intersection lines, such that a sector of an imaginary cylinder is defined between the longitudinal axis and the intersection lines. 23. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 16 to 22, wherein an angle described between the first contact and second contact surfaces is in the range of 10 degrees to 20 degrees. 24. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 23, wherein the angle described between the first contact and second contact surfaces is 15 degrees. 25. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, comprising twenty- four elements assembled together to form the ring structure. 26. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein at least some of the elements are provided with interlocking profiles for interlocking with an adjacent element. 27. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 26, wherein the interlocking profiles are formed in the first and/or second contact surfaces. 28. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein an element is configured to interlock with a contact surface of an adjacent element. 29. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 25 to 28, wherein interlocking prevents or restricts separation of assembled adjacent elements in a circumferential and/or radial direction of the ring structure, while enabling relative sliding movement of adjacent elements. 30. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein at least some of the elements assembled to form a ring are identical to one another. 31. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein each of the elements assembled to form a ring comprise an interlocking profile which is configured to interlock with a corresponding interlocking profile on another element. 32. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 31 , wherein the interlocking profiles comprise at least one recess and at least one protrusion configured to be received in the recess. 33. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 32, wherein the interlocking profiles comprise at least one dovetail recess and dovetail protrusion. 34. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the apparatus comprises a support surface for the ring structure. 35. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 34, wherein the support surface is the outer surface of a mandrel or tubular. 36. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 34 or 35, wherein the support surface supports the ring structure in a collapsed condition of the apparatus. 37. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 34, wherein the support surface is the inner surface of a mandrel or tubular. 38. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 37, wherein the support surface supports the ring structure in an expanded condition of the apparatus. 39. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the apparatus is operated in its expanded condition. 40. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 1 to 38, wherein the apparatus is operated in its collapsed condition. 41. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein elements forming the ring structure are mutually supportive in an operating condition of the apparatus. 42. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein when the operating condition of the apparatus is its expanded condition the ring structure is a substantially solid ring structure in its expanded condition. 43. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the apparatus comprises a formation configured to impart a radial expanding or collapsing force component to the elements of a ring structure from an axial actuation force. 44. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 43, wherein the formation comprises a wedge or wedge profile. 45. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 44, wherein the formation comprises a cone wedge or cone wedge profile. 46. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 43 to 45, wherein the apparatus comprises a pair of formations configured to impart a radial expanding or collapsing force component to the elements of a ring structure from an axial actuation force. 47. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 46, wherein at least one formation is configured to transmit torque to the ring structure. 48. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 43 to 47, wherein the at least one formation comprises a splined arrangement. 49. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the apparatus comprises a biasing means, configured to bias the ring structure to its collapsed condition or expanded condition. 50. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 49, wherein the biasing means comprises a circumferential spring, a garter spring, or a spiral retaining ring. 51. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 49 or 50, wherein the biasing means is arranged to bias the ring structure towards a collapsed condition. 52. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 49 or 50, wherein the biasing means is arranged to bias the ring structure towards an expanded condition. 53. The torque transfer apparatus according to any of claims 49 to 52, wherein one or more elements comprises a formation for receiving the biasing means. 54. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the elements comprise formations which combine to form a circumferential groove in the ring structure. 55. The torque transfer apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein multiple biasing means are provided on the ring structure. 56. A torque transfer apparatus comprising: a plurality of elements assembled together to form a ring structure oriented in a plane around a longitudinal axis and defining a first engagement surface; a structure defining a second engagement surface, wherein the structure is rotatable with respect to the ring structure; wherein the first engagement surface is a cylindrical surface; and wherein the ring structure is operable to be moved between first and second conditions to engage or disengage the first and second engagement surfaces. 57. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 56, wherein the first and second engagement surfaces are engaged to transfer a driving torque between the ring structure and the rotatable member. 58. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 57, wherein the torque transfer apparatus is a clutch. 59. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 56, wherein the first and second engagement surfaces are engaged to transfer a braking torque therebetween. 60. The torque transfer apparatus according to claim 59, wherein the torque transfer apparatus is a brake. 61. A method of transferring torque between engagement surfaces using the apparatus of any of claims 1 to 60. 62. A method of transferring a driving torque between engagement surfaces using the apparatus of any of claims 1 to 60. 63. A method of transferring a braking torque between engagement surfaces using the apparatus of any of claims 1 to 60.
